concerts.label: Jazz Concert with Sarah Vaughan, Erroll Garner, Lester Young and Others

works.t: Sometimes I Feel Like a Motherless Child

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: her daughter studies with Jimmy Carroll. [INTERVIEWER] Who is a former student of yours.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I said to Jimmy Carroll, who was an arranger, I said, 'Jimmy, how do you think it would sound if I put my snare drum on the top of this timpani?' ... and we started playing, and it sounded just like a field drum, and that's how we got that field drum sound on 'Colonel Bogie's March'.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Carroll was an associate of Mitch Miller. He not only did sing-a-longs... but he did a lot of the other pop dates... all sorts of people who came into Columbia under Mitch Miller’s reign.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
